project.name: Kirmya
project.mission: To help people who have recently lost their jobs regroup and find new opportunities, build meaningful professional connections, receive AI-powered career guidance, develop missing skills, obtain employee referrals, optimize resumes, prepare for interviews, analyze salaries, discover companies, track applications, grow professionally, and ultimately become the world's most trusted professional networking and career platform.
longTermGoals: Become a modern competitor in professional networking, communities, messaging, mentorship, learning, AI-powered career development, and recruitment.
Maintain a unique platform identity optimized for career recovery rather than copycatting LinkedIn, Indeed, Naukri, or Glassdoor.

frontend_stack.styling: Vanilla CSS & Emotion (Never use Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, Chakra UI, Ant Design, or competing CSS frameworks for new components)
guidelines: Subtle micro-animations (Framer Motion) prioritizing fade, slide, scale, ripple, and skeleton loading (avoid flashy motions)
Strict WCAG Accessibility (Semantic HTML, aria-label, focus states, keyboard navigation)
SEO best practices (Title tags, meta descriptions, correct heading hierarchy, single H1 per page)
Performance budgets (Lazy loading, code splitting, virtual lists, image optimization, optimized bundle sizes)

backend_stack.web_framework: Gin Framework (Target / Migrating from net/http Mux)
backend_stack.orm: GORM / SQL-ready ORM (Target / Migrating from raw SQL)
architecture: Repository Pattern & Service Layer with Dependency Injection
observability: Request IDs, Correlation IDs, and Structured Logging (slog)
api_design: Cursor Pagination, Filtering, Sorting, and input validations

coding_standards: SOLID Principles, Clean Architecture, DRY, KISS, YAGNI
Strict ESLint, Prettier, and TypeScript checks
Gofmt compliance and proper Go code structuring
No duplicate code, no unused code, no unnecessary dependencies
Zero TODOs or placeholder implementations in committed code
Zero mock APIs unless explicitly requested
Zero console errors or TypeScript compilation warnings

security_standards: JWT Authentication with rotated, family-revocable httpOnly Refresh Token cookies
Content Security Policy (CSP) Headers and strict CORS
RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) and Audit Logging
OWASP Top 10 compliance: input validation, output encoding, password hashing (argon2), secure file uploads

rules: Always update frontend, backend, tests, swagger/OpenAPI spec, database migrations, and .env.example together in a single coordinated change.
Always produce complete, production-ready enterprise-grade implementations rather than partial or placeholder solutions.
Strictly preserve backward compatibility for existing APIs and models.
Ensure all new interactive elements have unique, descriptive IDs for browser automation testing.
Maintain documentation parity across memory.md, settings.json, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md.
